people do not decide their futures, they decide their habits and their habits decide their futures. f. m. alexander. archived post.
there are only two ways to live your life. one is as though nothing is a miracle. the other is as though everything is a miracle.
begin at once to live, and count each separate day as a separate life.
the wise man can safely control himself without becoming over-anxious; he can halt his tears and his pleasures at will.
we suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
life ultimately means taking the responsibility to find the right answer to its problems and to fulfill the tasks which it constantly sets for each individual.
success is nothing more than a few simple disciplines, practiced every day; while failure is simply a few errors in judgment, repeated every day.
the late jim rohn talks about on the power of adopting your own personal philosophy. “the formula for success is a few simple disciplines practiced every day.
f. m. alexander — 'people do not decide their futures, they decide their habits and their habits decide their futures.
an easy & proven way to build good habits & break bad ones. you do not rise to the level of your goals. you fall to the level of your systems.
- seneca live a life of restraint and respect for others. avoid harm and wrongdoing. we should cultivate a peaceful mind and a clear conscience.
most powerful is he who has himself in his own power." – seneca true strength lies in mastering oneself, not in controlling others.
epictetus one said "no man is free who is not master of himself. a man should so live that his happiness shall depend as little as possible on external things.
waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. be one.
you can be satisfied anytime your system is running. and a system can be successful in many different forms, not just the one you first envision." - james clear.
you have power over your mind, not outside events. realize this, and you will find strength.
